content:
Today, James incubates early-stage ventures — including, at one point, an estate agency built just to sell his own house — alongside creative and experimental projects, all whilst continuing to run camalyn.

Recent projects
justwitter.com
A public voice feed — record and share up to three minutes, unedited, for anyone to hear. Voices, not posts.
justvoiceit.com
90-second voice messages sent without a name attached. No one knows it’s you.
just2voices.com
Random one-to-one voice calls between strangers. Nothing recorded, nothing stored, no identities exchanged.
justswearme.com
Push a button to be sworn at. Or record your own — one gets published each week.
bekkett.com
Create a room, share the code, and speak freely — no names, no accounts, just whoever shows up.
join1215.com
Claim a seal instead of a name, and broadcast under it consistently: a voice people can follow and come back to.
kaiunta.com
Voice feedback for organisations — a simple prompt, an honest spoken answer, nothing traced back to who said it.
ladderloop.com
A one-button arcade game — tap to climb, fall back down, and repeat, in an endless loop.
sonialam.art
A single ambient portrait — a slowly drifting photograph with a name that fades in, and optional ambient sound.
Shelved projects (not every experiment is meant to run forever)
A trading platform exploring ideas around social value; an early attempt at reimagining job hunting around direct conversation rather than resumes and filters; and another exploring job ads as images rather than text.
Historic projects
A phpBB forum (drwho.tv) for Doctor Who fans, and an early attempt at a new way to pay for content.
